outcome_contract.why: When a guest asks about something the hostel has no template for, the system selects the closest BM25 match and sends it as if it were correct. Staff should only see genuinely tricky cases — not false alarms from a missing threshold gate or spurious reference-link failures on routine FAQ emails.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: Per-question confidence floor and category-compatibility check gate template block acceptance in `draft-generate`; below-threshold single-question emails emit `follow_up_required` with the escalation fallback sentence and no template text. `booking-issues` removed from `STRICT_REFERENCE_CATEGORIES` to eliminate false reference-link quality failures on FAQ drafts.

engineering_coverage.applicable: `follow_up_required = true` surfaces in draft output for below-threshold single-Q emails; staff see escalation sentence instead of wrong template
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Options B/C: classifier changes could produce different `follow_up_required` patterns across both paths
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: `follow_up_required` on below-threshold single-Q emails; quality check no longer fails FAQ drafts with `booking-issues` tag
N/A — no auth, PII, or exposure changes

engineering_coverage.applicable: `usedTemplateFallback = true` fires for below-threshold single-Q emails; `email_fallback_detected` telemetry emitted; learning ledger written
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Telemetry gap closed: "suggest-but-wrong" events now recorded alongside "none" events

engineering_coverage.applicable: New unit tests: single-Q floor gate, category-compatibility rejection, `booking-issues` false reference failure regression
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Option B/C would require broader classifier test coverage
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Three new test cases; extends existing `draft-generate.test.ts` and `draft-quality-check.test.ts` patterns

engineering_coverage.applicable: `TemplateRankResult` does not need a new field — category-compatibility check runs in caller using existing `candidates[0].template.category`; `usedTemplateFallback` semantics unchanged (still `!selectedTemplate`)
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: No schema changes; existing telemetry field reused

engineering_coverage.applicable: Category-compatibility check is a single conditional on already-resolved `candidates[0]` — negligible
Backwards-compatible; no deploy flag; rollback is revert of two files (`draft-generate.ts`, `draft-quality-check.ts`)

stage_payload.decision_summary: Two independent code gaps allow the email draft pipeline to produce misleading outputs. This analysis decides how to close each gap — the choices are independent and can be implemented in sequence. **Decision 1:** How to gate single-question template selection so only confidently-matched templates are used (mirroring the composite path's existing protection). **Decision 2:** How to eliminate false `missing_required_reference` quality failures on FAQ drafts that incidentally mention a booking. Both decisions are structurally simple: the composite path already solves Decision 1 correctly, and `b
goals: Gate single-question template acceptance with a category-compatibility check and confidence floor matching the composite path.
Emit `follow_up_required` with the escalation fallback sentence for below-threshold single-question emails (no template text in draft).
Remove `booking-issues` from `STRICT_REFERENCE_CATEGORIES` so informational booking mentions do not trigger reference-link enforcement.
non_goals: Changing the BM25 algorithm, synonym table, or phrase expansions.
Altering the multi-question composite path — it already gates correctly.
constraints: Tests run in CI only per `docs/testing-policy.md`.
All changes confined to `packages/mcp-server/src/`. No schema migrations, environment variables, or data files required.
The composite path must remain behaviourally unchanged.
assumptions: The "suggest-but-wrong" production cases observed in the operator audit resulted from single-question path processing (consistent with code analysis).
`booking_action_required` correctly covers all cases where a booking reference URL is genuinely required; no booking-action case will lose enforcement.

evaluation_criteria.Why it matters: The fix should produce identical gating logic as the already-correct path
Other paths, the ranker internals, and multi-question behaviour must be unaffected
The new gate must be directly unit-testable without integration setup
Staff reviewers need a signal when the AI couldn't answer
Fewer new abstractions are better; reuse existing constants and helper patterns

options_considered.Option: A — Mirror composite-path logic inline in `handleDraftGenerateTool`
options_considered.Description: Add a local gate after `rankTemplates` returns: check `rankResult.candidates[0]` category-hint compatibility first; if incompatible or below `UNHINTED_TEMPLATE_CONFIDENCE_FLOOR = 70`, clear `selectedTemplate` and set `followUpRequired = true`. Reuse existing constants.
options_considered.Upside: No new helper required; logic mirrors composite path exactly; composite path unchanged; `usedTemplateFallback = true` naturally covers the new path
options_considered.Key risks: Existing hint-check logic must be faithfully copied — a unit test enforces this

options_considered.Description: Push category-awareness down into `template-ranker.ts` so `rankTemplates` returns `categoryCompatible` and a higher threshold for unmatched hints
options_considered.Upside: Ranker becomes self-sufficient; callers get structured signal
options_considered.Downside: Template-ranker currently has no concept of scenario categories; adds cross-cutting coupling; ranker is shared by multi-question path — must not break it
options_considered.Key risks: Risk of unintended side effects on composite path; harder to test in isolation

options_considered.Description: Single constant change eliminates "suggest"-level results entirely
options_considered.Downside: Changes multi-question path behaviour; eliminates "suggest"-level results that currently work well for borderline matches
options_considered.Key risks: Multi-question path uses `PER_QUESTION_FLOOR = 25` separately — raising `SUGGEST_THRESHOLD` would create inconsistency

options_considered.Description: One-line deletion from the string set constant
options_considered.Upside: Zero new logic; `booking_action_required` continues to enforce links for actual booking actions; consistent with how `faq` and `general` are exempted
options_considered.Key risks: Assumes all cases needing reference enforcement are already covered by `booking_action_required` (verified: yes)

options_considered.Option: B — Narrow `booking-issues` classifier regex to exclude informational patterns
options_considered.Description: Edit `draft-interpret.ts` to tighten the patterns at lines 875–879 and 805–808
options_considered.Downside: Classifier changes carry wider blast radius (all consumers); harder to test exhaustively; doesn't fix the root coupling between scenario tag and reference enforcement
options_considered.Key risks: Classifier changes could introduce new false negatives in scenarios that do require action

options_considered.Option: C — Add a `reference_required` sub-flag to `booking-issues` scenario classification
options_considered.Description: Add context-sensitive reference requirement at classification time
options_considered.Downside: Requires new contract field, downstream changes in `draft-quality-check.ts`, and significant test surface expansion
options_considered.Key risks: Over-engineers a simple misconfiguration; the simpler fix (Option A) is fully correct given `booking_action_required` coverage

chosen_approach.Recommendation: Apply both Option A decisions as a single bounded change: (1) add a post-`rankTemplates` category-compatibility and confidence-floor gate in `handleDraftGenerateTool`'s single-question path, mirroring `selectQuestionTemplateCandidate` semantics; (2) remove `booking-issues` from `STRICT_REFERENCE_CATEGORIES` in `draft-quality-check.ts`.
chosen_approach.Decision 1: mirrors the already-validated composite-path logic exactly — same constants, same semantics, no new abstractions. `usedTemplateFallback` naturally handles the telemetry gap.
chosen_approach.Decision 2: the root cause is a misconfigured constant (`booking-issues` in the strict set), not a classifier flaw. `booking_action_required` already provides the correct enforcement path. The fix is one-line with zero blast radius.
chosen_approach.`policyCandidates[0]?.template.category`: ScenarioCategory` at template-ranker.ts:111. `policyCandidates` is the post-policy-constraint version of `rankResult.candidates` and must be used (not `rankResult.candidates[0]`) to stay consistent with existing code.

rejected_approaches: Option B (extend ranker) — adds category-awareness to a layer that is correctly category-agnostic; carries risk of side effects on the composite path.
Option C (raise `SUGGEST_THRESHOLD`) — changes multi-question behaviour and creates inconsistency with `PER_QUESTION_FLOOR`.
Option B for Decision 2 (narrow classifier regex) — wider blast radius than the problem requires; the coupling is in `STRICT_REFERENCE_CATEGORIES`, not in the classifier.
Option C for Decision 2 (sub-flag on booking-issues) — over-engineers a simple misconfiguration.

sequencing_constraints: TASK-01 and TASK-02 are independent — can be implemented in either order or in parallel.
No CHECKPOINT required — both tasks are small, risk-bounded, and unit-testable.
risks_to_carry_forward: Category-compatibility gate logic must mirror `selectQuestionTemplateCandidate` semantics exactly — the unit test for TASK-01 is the primary correctness signal.
The `usedTemplateFallback` flag semantics must not change for existing callers (`!selectedTemplate` is still the expression — no downstream consumer changes required).

areas.label: Single-question draft generation
areas.current: `rankTemplates` returns "suggest"-level candidate; `handleDraftGenerateTool` uses it as template body regardless of category match
areas.trigger: Guest asks about topic with no template (e.g. rooftop pool)
areas.future: (1) `rankTemplates` returns `selection: "suggest"`, `candidates[0]`; (2) new gate checks category-hint compatibility; (3) if incompatible or `confidence < UNHINTED_TEMPLATE_CONFIDENCE_FLOOR`, `selectedTemplate = undefined`, `usedTemplateFallback = true`; (4) escalation fallback sentence injected; (5) `email_fallback_detected` telemetry emitted; (6) learning ledger written
areas.steady: Multi-question composite path — unchanged; hard-rule categories (`prepayment`, `cancellation`) — unchanged; `selection === "none"` path — unchanged (already falls through to fallback)
areas.seams: Category-compatibility check must exactly mirror `selectQuestionTemplateCandidate` semantics — unit test must cover both `category-match + low-confidence` and `no-category-match + high-confidence` cases

areas.label: FAQ draft quality check
areas.current: FAQ draft with "booking inquiry" phrasing gets `booking-issues` scenario tag; `requiresReferenceForActionPlan` enforces reference URL; draft fails `missing_required_reference`
areas.trigger: Guest sends FAQ email with incidental booking mention
areas.future: (1) `booking-issues` scenario tag still assigned by classifier; (2) `requiresReferenceForActionPlan` checks `STRICT_REFERENCE_CATEGORIES` — `booking-issues` no longer present; (3) reference check skipped for `booking-issues` unless `bookingActionRequired === true`; (4) quality check passes for plain FAQ draft
areas.steady: `booking_action_required` enforcement via `missing_required_link` check — unchanged; `cancellation`, `prepayment`, `payment`, `policies`, `booking-changes` in `STRICT_REFERENCE_CATEGORIES` — unchanged
areas.seams: None — the only dependency is that `booking_action_required` correctly covers all booking-action cases, which is confirmed
